whatsNew: Close the gap between knowing the rules and following them. The reference demo looks designed because it went through a loop — lock the look, build, run the quality gate, fix to a floor, repeat — that a free-hand build silently skips. New /ss-build makes that loop one command: it refuses to write UI before the look is locked in STYLESEED.md, then scores and fixes to ≥80 before showing you anything. And the one-paste prompt now bootstraps its own install (npx skills add bitjaru/styleseed) first — because /ss-score and /ss-build can only *run* when installed; point an agent at the rules-URL alone and the gate degrades to an honor-system self-check it skips. Install-first = the gate is real, the lock persists, output stops looking generated.
update: Run /ss-update, or: npx skills add bitjaru/styleseed
